rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the model or imported packages before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the correct scalar types (int, real, bool) and ensuring binary operations use compatible types.
Address 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by removing or correcting clauses that reference invalid identifiers or undeclared helpers on the same line.
Correct 'does not point to anything' diagnostics by verifying that property associations and terms reference valid AADL elements within the current scope.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers and types are visible in the current scope and properly declared in the model.
Validate that expressions in AGREE contracts maintain type consistency to prevent runtime or validation errors.
Use only visible AADL features or helpers declared within the same annex when writing assumptions or guarantees.
Avoid referencing implementation-level subcomponents or ports in component type annexes; move such bindings to implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Helper declarations in AGREE must specify correct scalar types (int, real, bool) to match their assigned expressions.
Binary comparisons in AGREE require operands of the same type; use appropriate type casting or declarations to ensure compatibility.
Property references in AGREE must resolve to valid definitions or constants declared in the AADL model or imported packages.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references that are not present in the analyzed model or its dependencies.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they reference visible features or outputs.
Do not write assumptions or guarantees that reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations without fixing the root cause.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean or opaque data types into numeric scalars for range comparisons unless the AADL model explicitly supports such conversion.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and matching with clauses
Component type vs implementation scope placement of contracts
